reasoning: The indicators provide a confluent bullish signal for equities. The US Treasury yield curve is upward sloping (normal), which signifies market expectations of future economic expansion [57205ed3e4b4]. Simultaneously, the 10-year real interest rate is in the 4th percentile (low relative to history), which is expected to increase liquidity, reduce volatility in risk assets [99ad6cdde172], and lead to expansionary GDP [239bc87b8f03, 2328dabf508c]. This low real rate environment is consistent with the observed normal/suppressed VIX (39th percentile), as very low real rates are associated with suppressed volatility [0826c79434b2]. All three indicators align to suggest an environment supportive of risk assets.
